req.title: Manager creates department
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three primary test dimensions: (1) a happy-path where an authenticated manager submits a fully valid form and receives a 200/201 with an entity id plus an audit record (EP – valid partition); (2) a negative path where mandatory fields are omitted, triggering a 400 validation error (NEG / DT – the decision table row where required fields are absent); (3) a boundary case probing the maximum allowed length of the department name field (BVA – exact boundary value). Additional negative cases cover unauthenticated access (401) and forbidden role (403) to exercise the full error-pathway matrix. State Transition Coverage is applied to confirm the entity lifecycle: non-existent → created → audit-recorded.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_department_valid_payload
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <fixture:manager_token>
POST /departments with a fully populated, valid JSON body
Capture the response status code and body
Query the audit log for the created department id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
An audit record exists for the returned department id
trace: R-WEB-005: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_audit_record

@pytest.mark.parametrize("dept_name", [f"Engineering-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"])
def test_manager_creates_department_valid_payload(manager_token, dept_name):
    payload = {
        "name": dept_name,
        "code": "ENG",
        "location": "HQ"
    }
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/departments",
        json=payload,
        token=manager_token
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    dept_id = body.get("id")
    assert dept_id, "Response must contain a non-empty 'id'"
    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=dept_id, action="CREATE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for created department"

tests.name: test_create_department_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <fixture:manager_token>
POST /departments with an empty JSON body (all mandatory fields absent)
Capture the response status code and body
expected: Response body contains validation error details
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_create_department_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(manager_token):
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/departments",
        json={},
        token=manager_token
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 for empty payload, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "errors" in body or "message" in body, (
        "Response must include validation error details"
    )

tests.name: test_create_department_name_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Maximum allowed department name length is 100 characters (per field spec)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <fixture:manager_token>
Construct a department name of exactly 100 characters
Also POST with a name of 101 characters and verify rejection
trace: R-WEB-005: department is created (at exact boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

MAX_LEN = 100

def test_create_department_name_at_max_boundary_length(manager_token):
    name_ok = "D" * MAX_LEN
    resp_ok = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/departments",
        json={"name": name_ok, "code": "BVA", "location": "HQ"},
        token=manager_token
    )
    assert resp_ok.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"100-char name should be accepted, got {resp_ok.status_code}"
    )
    name_over = "D" * (MAX_LEN + 1)
    resp_over = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/departments",
        json={"name": name_over, "code": "BVA", "location": "HQ"},
        token=manager_token
    )
    assert resp_over.status_code == 400, (
        f"101-char name should be rejected, got {resp_over.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_create_department_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /departments with a valid payload but without an Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_create_department_unauthenticated_returns_401():
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/departments",
        json={"name": "Ghost Dept", "code": "GHO", "location": "HQ"},
        token=None
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated request, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_create_department_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated as a non-manager role (e.g. 'employee')
actions: Obtain an auth token for a user with 'employee' role via <fixture:employee_token>
POST /departments with a valid payload using the employee token
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_create_department_forbidden_role_returns_403(employee_token):
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/departments",
        json={"name": "Forbidden Dept", "code": "FBD", "location": "HQ"},
        token=employee_token
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for non-manager role, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
